'''Jack Wells''' (born 21 September 1997) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref name="Super League: Wakefield Wildcats 62-0 Wigan Warriors">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/35970702|title=Super League: Wakefield Wildcats 62-0 Wigan Warriors|publisher=BBC|date=2016-04-10|accessdate=2016-09-13}}</ref>

He played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the Super League, and spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at both the [[Swinton Lions]] and the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[2019 RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].{{fact|date=January 2024}}

==Background==
Wells was born in [[Salford]], Greater Manchester, England.{{fact|date=January 2024}}

==Playing career==
===Wigan Warriors===
He has spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at both the [[Swinton Lions]] and [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[2019 RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].{{fact|date=January 2024}}

===Salford Red Devils===
On 16 December 2020 it was announced that Wells would be joining the [[Salford Red Devils]] for the 2021 season; his hometown club and the club he supported as a boy. He left the Red Devils after disappointing in super league dropping down to play part time rugby. His brother Daniel became involved in a scandal.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55330529|title=Jack Wells: Salford sign forward following exit from Wigan Warriors|work=BBC Sport |date=16 December 2020 |accessdate=16 Dec 2020}}</ref>
